name: Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP)
applicable_scenarios: derive criteria weights from expert pairwise comparisons
hierarchical decision problems with goal -> criteria -> alternatives
structuring subjective judgments into a consistent weight vector
python_template:
import numpy as np

def ahp_weights(A):
    # A: n x n pairwise-comparison matrix with a[i][j] = importance of i over j.
    A = np.asarray(A, dtype=float)
    eigvals, eigvecs = np.linalg.eig(A)
    idx = int(np.argmax(eigvals.real))
    lam_max = float(eigvals.real[idx])
    w = np.abs(eigvecs[:, idx].real)
    w = w / w.sum()
    n = A.shape[0]
    ci = (lam_max - n) / (n - 1) if n > 1 else 0.0
    ri_table = {1: 0.0, 2: 0.0, 3: 0.58, 4: 0.90, 5: 1.12, 6: 1.24,
                7: 1.32, 8: 1.41, 9: 1.45, 10: 1.49}
    ri = ri_table.get(n, 1.49)
    cr = ci / ri if ri > 0 else 0.0
    return w, lam_max, cr
typical_cases: weighting criteria for a TOPSIS-style composite score
prioritizing project alternatives under expert judgment
policy choice with qualitative + quantitative criteria
common_pitfalls: consistency ratio CR should be < 0.1; otherwise revisit the matrix
pairwise judgments don't scale past ~9 criteria (cognitive load)
rank reversal when alternatives change — known critique
garbage-in-garbage-out: weights are only as good as the expert elicitation
